claim_partition: holdout_results only; calibration_diagnostics are descriptive and never pooled with holdout
interval_method: Boundary-safe 95% intervals: Wilson for track proportions, Garwood Poisson count-rate intervals for alert episodes, and distribution-free bounded-track Hoeffding intervals for ARL0 and post-warm-up abstention, conservatively enveloped by whole-track bootstrap when >=80% of 500 requested replicates are usable; whole-track percentile bootstrap for delay
sensitivity_axes: clean_autocorrelation varies AR(1) phi at covariance_scale=1
clean_covariance_sensitivity varies declared covariance scale at phi=0; clean_autocorrelation phi=0 is the scale=1 reference

false_alerts_per_hour.estimator: pooled alert episodes / pooled track exposure
false_alerts_per_hour.interval: 95% Garwood exact Poisson count-rate interval under a homogeneous episode-rate model; whole-track bootstrap envelope used only when at least 80% of replicates are usable (500 usable of 500 requested)

mission_probability_any_alert.interval: 95% Wilson score interval for a track-level binomial proportion; whole-track bootstrap envelope used only when at least 80% of replicates are usable (500 usable of 500 requested)

arl0_assessments.estimator: restricted mean time to first alert; right-censored at each track end
arl0_assessments.interval: 95% distribution-free Hoeffding interval for track-level time-to-first-alert bounded by the configured assessment horizon; whole-track bootstrap envelope used only when at least 80% of replicates are usable (500 usable of 500 requested)

arl0_censoring_fraction.interval: 95% Wilson score interval for a track-level binomial proportion; whole-track bootstrap envelope used only when at least 80% of replicates are usable (500 usable of 500 requested)

abstention_fraction.estimator: post-warm-up pooled insufficient-or-rejected assessments / pooled monitoring assessments
abstention_fraction.interval: 95% distribution-free weighted-track Hoeffding interval for bounded post-warm-up abstention fractions; whole-track bootstrap envelope used only when at least 80% of replicates are usable (500 usable of 500 requested)

conditional_detection_probability.estimator: detected tracks / tracks without a pre-onset alert

conditional_delay_frames.estimator: median first post-onset alert delay among detected tracks without pre-onset alert

conditional_attribution_accuracy.estimator: exact first-alert channel attribution among detected tracks without pre-onset alert

status_reasons: insufficient_duration: 15800 ms is below configured minimum 3600000 ms

limitations: Synthetic observations are controlled stress tests, not a deployed residual population or operational accuracy claim.
Configured family_alpha is a per-assessment family-wise bound under the detector model; it is not a stream false-alert-rate guarantee.

Garwood episode-rate intervals assume a homogeneous Poisson count process; whole-track bootstrap envelopes are reported when sufficiently estimable, but neither turns the controlled stream into an operational FAR claim.
ARL0 is a finite-horizon restricted mean and must be read with its censoring fraction.
Attack delay and attribution are conditional on no pre-onset alert; delay is also conditional on detection.
Alert episodes use the configured nominal_only reset policy: abstention preserves an active episode, and only a nominal assessment clears it.
The default runner evaluates streaming NIS and signed correlation only; PID has no product streaming cadence in this revision.
